help.intro: Secrets store a credential once — an API key, password, or token — so you reference it by name instead of pasting the raw value everywhere it's needed.
help.step1: Create a named secret here and enter its value.
help.step2: Reference it as {{secret:NAME}} in a server's upstream authentication, headers, or query parameters.
help.step3: Reveal or copy the value only when you deliberately choose to, through its own action — the value never renders in the list.
help.twoLayers: There are two separate authentication layers: the credentials Arthur uses to call your upstream API (Secrets, here), and the credentials an AI client uses to call your MCP server (Access Keys or OAuth, configured per server under Connect). Keep them mentally separate.
help.troubleshootingReveal: If you can't reveal a value, confirm your role has the reveal permission — viewing names and creating secrets is a separate, more common permission.

create.title: New secret
create.detailsHint: Use a stable uppercase name because servers reference it by name.
create.valueHint: The value is only requested here and is not returned in list or detail metadata.
create.reviewHint: Confirm the reference syntax before storing the secret.

placeholder.description: What this secret is used for…

hint.nameChangeWarning: Changing the name will break all existing {{secret:OLD_NAME}} references.
hint.valueProtected: Value is encrypted and cannot be retrieved
hint.deleteBreaksServers: This will break all servers using this secret.

empty.noSecrets: No secrets yet. Click New secret to add one.
empty.noSecretsSimple: No secrets yet. Click New secret to add one.
empty.noActivity: No activity recorded for this secret.

confirm.deleteMessage: This secret will be permanently removed. Auth configs using it will stop working.
confirm.deleteSwalText: This will permanently remove the secret. All servers using it will stop working.
